title: Hash Tables, Dictionaries, Sets, and Collision Policy · Spiral Review
summary: Create a deduplication and lookup service that remains correct under collision-heavy data. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a hash-table model and workload benchmark after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
Trace separate chaining and open addressing through collision and deletion cases.
Explain how load factor and resizing influence expected cost and memory.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
